nodes.name: Google Gemini Chat Model
nodes.notes: This lmChatGoogleGemini node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Basic LLM Chain

parameters.text:
=Your Task is to find the best resources to learn {{ $('GetTopicFromToLearn').item.json["I want to learn"] }}. 

I have scraped the HackerNews and The following is the list of comments from HackerNews on topic about Learning {{ $('GetTopicFromToLearn').item.json["I want to learn"] }}


Focus only on comments that provide any resouces or advice or insight about learning {{ $('GetTopicFromToLearn').item.json.Learn }}. Ignore all other comments that are off topic discussions.

Now based on these comments, you need to find the top resources and list them. 

Categorize them based on resource type (course, book, article, youtube videos, lectures, etc) and also figure out the difficultiy level (beginner, intermediate, advanced, expert).

You don't always to have fill in these categories exactly, these are given here for reference. Use your intution to find the best categorization.

Now based on these metrics and running a basic sentiment analysis on comments you need to figure out what the top resources are. 

Respond back in Markdown formatted text. In the following format

**OUTPUT FORMAT**

```

## Top HN Recomended Resources To Learn <topic Name> 

### Category 1

- **Resource 1** - One line description
- **Resource 2** - One line description
- ... 

<add hyperlinks if any exists>

### Category 2

- **Resource 1** - One line description
- **Resource 2** - One line description
- ... 

<add hyperlinks in markdown format to the resource name itself if any exists. Example [resource name]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }}>

...
```

Here is the list of HackerNews Comments.

{{ $json.text }}

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: google Palm  A P I details
configurationSteps: Verify the settings of the trigger node "GetTopicFromToLearn".
Open the "Google Gemini Chat Model" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Basic LLM Chain" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SearchAskHN" node (n8n-nodes-base.hackerNews) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "FindHNComments" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "CombineIntoSingleText" node (n8n-nodes-base.aggregate) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SplitOutChildrenIDs" node (n8n-nodes-base.splitOut) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "SendEmailWithTopResources" node (n8n-nodes-base.emailSend) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Convert2HTML" node (n8n-nodes-base.markdown) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
Open the "Finished" node (n8n-nodes-base.noOp) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.
No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
